summary: Soaring Peggy‑O, unstoppable Terrapin>UJB stretch & tight jams
blurb: The show delivers a soaring Peggy‑O solo and a high‑energy Uncle John's Band into The Wheel sequence. Highlights include a monster Sugaree, an unbroken Terrapin Station > Playing in the Band run, and a vibrant second‑set flow.
review: May 19, 1977 at Atlanta’s Fox Theater captures one of the spring ’77 high points – a night where the Dead were firing on all cylinders. Jerry Garcia roared through a visceral Peggy‑O solo that many fans call “the best ever,” and his smoky, sweet tone on Sugaree set the tone for an evening of relentless groove. The second set is a textbook example of ’77 magic: Terrapin Station seamlessly into Playing In The Band, which barrels straight into Uncle John's Band, then rolls through a tight Drums‑The Wheel‑China Doll segment that never loses momentum. Bob Weir’s early licks on Peggy‑O “kill those early licks,” and his vocal push on Dancin’ in the Street adds extra sparkle. Keith Godchaux’s piano shines early on, especially under the Sugaree groove, while Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann keep a solid, propulsive foundation that drives The Wheel and the extended jams. Across all four soundboard sources the performance is described as “legendary,” “on fire,” and “unstoppable,” making this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead seeking a quintessential ’77 experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the same standout moments (Peggy‑O solo, Sugaree, Terrapin > Playing in the Band stretch) with no negative commentary, indicating strong consensus of an exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with only minor splice/hiss quirks; preserves the soaring Peggy‑O solo and high‑energy UJB/Playing in the Band flow.

key_highlights: Visceral, best‑ever Peggy‑O solo that launches the set.
Monster Sugaree that anchors both sets with smoky guitar work.
Uninterrupted Terrapin Station > Playing In The Band > Uncle John's Band marathon.
Seamless second‑set flow through Drums, The Wheel and China Doll.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a visceral Peggy‑O solo, sweet smoky tone on Sugaree, wizardry throughout, and a tight soulful Estimated Prophet improvisation – essentially ‘on fire’ all night.
band_performance.Bob: Provides strong rhythm/vocal support, early licks that “kill those early licks” on Peggy‑O, and backs Jerry perfectly; his vocal push on Dancin' in the Street adds extra drive.
band_performance.Keith: Clear, bright piano shines early in the set, especially under the Sugaree groove, adding melodic depth to the performance.
band_performance.Mickey: Solid drumming that drives The Wheel and supports the relaxed vibe; while not wildly flashy, provides a reliable backbone.
band_performance.Billy: Provides steady, propulsive percussion alongside Mickey, keeping the momentum tight through extended jams.
